subject: LACS
course_description: Like most peoples in world history, Jews have been victims of enslavement as well as enslavers and owners of enslaved people. Students in this course will analyze Jewish sources and subjects to study slavery as a historical phenomenon in one of the pre-eminent Jewish communities of the Middle East.
course_notes: (Same as HIST 264W 1 and MESAS 264W 1) (From the biblical period to the modern-era, Israelites and Jews were both victims of enslavement as well as enslavers and owners of enslaved people. Students in this course will analyze Jewish sources and subjects to study slavery as a world-historical phenomenon in one of the pre-eminent Jewish communities of the premodern world: medieval Cairo in Islamic Egypt. Egyptian Jews preserved hundreds of records that illuminate the history of slavery. Jews in Cairo purchased slaves from Nubia, India, and Europe. Egyptian rabbis, including the polymath Moses Maimonides, wrote copiously about the Jewish laws of slavery as well as the Jewish men who sometimes defied these regulations. Recurring themes in this course include race and ethnicity; gender and sex; child slavery; slavery and the law; religious conversion; and the experiences of enslaved and freed people.)
